modeRationale: Existing site is fully dark across all pages. Their identity is a dark-substrate fight gym — keeping the dark mode is correct. The choice is HOW dark, not WHETHER dark.

colorValidation.logoColor: #BC6025 — burnt-orange/copper medallion. This is THE brand color.

validatedColors.#BC6025: CONFIRMED — exact logo medallion color, appears in CTA buttons and active nav state
validatedColors.#000000: CONFIRMED — page background, navbar background, footer background
validatedColors.#171717: CONFIRMED — card and elevated surface background
validatedColors.#5A5D65: CONFIRMED — muted text and disabled state color
validatedColors.#FFFFFF: CONFIRMED — primary body text on dark

rejectedColors.#6a8161: REJECTED as a primary brand color. Found only as a leftover CSS custom property (--accent) in their Next.js stylesheet but does NOT appear in the logo, navbar, or any primary CTA in the rendered UI. Likely a Tailwind default or an old design system artifact. Demoting to a tertiary 'success / active-duty pill' accent only.

colorValidation.note: All retained colors appear in the logo OR the navbar OR the primary CTA — no false positives from background photography.

colorAudit.primaryAccent: #BC6025 — burnt orange / copper. Saturation 67%, lightness 44%. Reads as 'oxidized copper' or 'fight-gear leather' — perfect register for a combat sports gym. Not the cliché UFC-red, not the cliché safety-orange — distinctive.

contrastResults.verdict: Passes WCAG AA for large text and graphical UI elements. Acceptable for headlines, badges, buttons.
Passes AA Large. Use for CTAs and headline accents.
AAA. Pure white on near-black is fine but slightly harsh — soften to #F4F4F4 for body text to reduce eye strain.
FAILS AA for body text. Lift muted-text color to #9CA0A8 (6.7:1).

colorAudit.harmonyNote: Single chromatic accent (burnt orange) on a near-black greyscale substrate. This is the ideal palette for the cinematic-fight register — the orange does ALL the chromatic work, every other color stays in the warm-grey-to-black ramp. No clashing.
colorAudit.hierarchyStatus: EXCELLENT once corrected. Single accent + cool greyscale gives clean signal-to-noise.
colorAudit.saturationNote: Primary accent at 67% saturation is properly tuned — not neon, not muddy. Holds up against both dark backgrounds and behind oversized portrait photography.
warnings: Pure #000 background is harsh on OLED displays and creates banding in image gradients. Use #0A0A0A.
Original muted-text #5A5D65 fails AA for body copy on near-black. Lifted to #9CA0A8 for body, kept #5A5D65 for true subtle/disabled states only.
Pure #FFFFFF text on near-black is technically fine but feels harsh in a cinematic register — soften to #F4F4F4 for body, keep #FFFFFF for high-priority headlines only.

paletteRoles.successPill: #6A8161 (only used on Active Duty pricing badge and 'available' indicators — keeps the sage from going to waste while never letting it compete with the burnt-orange accent)

usageRules: BURNT-ORANGE #BC6025 IS PRECIOUS. Use it for: primary CTAs, hover state on links, the medallion logo, headline accent words, the moving marquee bar, the 'Where Fighters Are Made' line, and one or two number-callouts. Nothing else.
Never use orange for body text. Never put two orange elements within 200px of each other unless they're part of a deliberate cluster (like a CTA group).
Never use the sage (#6A8161) as a primary accent. Sage appears ONLY on the Active Duty pricing pill ($180/mo · ACTIVE DUTY) and on 'available' indicators in the schedule. That's it.
Backgrounds use the #0A0A0A → #141414 → #1C1C1C elevation ramp. Anything brighter on the dark substrate needs a clear reason.
Pure #FFFFFF only for the very top headline of the page and the logo wordmark. Body text is #F4F4F4 to reduce harshness.
Borders are #2D2D2D for default, #3A3A3A for emphasized (hover, focus, active card).
Drop shadows: avoid. Use border-strong or glow rings instead — dark substrate doesn't shadow well.
Glow: a soft #BC6025 glow at 0.32 opacity behind the primary hero CTA only. Optional on hover for instructor portrait cards.
colorShifts: Substrate softened from pure #000000 to #0A0A0A (less harsh on OLED, allows for image gradient blending)
Card surface raised from #171717 to #141414 (slightly closer to bg so cards feel embedded in the obsidian, not floating)
Muted text raised from #5A5D65 to #9CA0A8 (fixes WCAG AA contrast failure for body)
Pure #FFFFFF reserved for top headlines only; body text uses #F4F4F4 (reduces eye strain, feels more cinematic)
Added hover variant #D17434 (15% lighter than primary) for accent interaction states
Added pressed variant #9F4F1C (15% darker) for button :active states
Demoted #6A8161 sage from CSS-variable artifact to a single-use 'Active Duty pill' accent — it's preserved but never competes with the burnt-orange

fontRecommendation.displayUse: Editorial display serif for hero headlines, section openers, founder-story callouts. Use opsz axis for very large display sizes (set opsz to 144 at 80px+), and dial WONK to 1 on the largest hero word for a touch of personality without going full art-house. Italic for pull quotes only.
fontRecommendation.bodyUse: Utilitarian sans for body, nav, buttons, captions, and all UI. Letter-spacing slightly tightened (-0.01em) on small text. All-caps eyebrow labels use Inter 600 with +0.12em tracking.
fontRecommendation.monoUse: Spec-sheet numerals on instructor credential blocks (8-5 record, BJJ Brown Belt, 25 yrs), class times on schedule, the marquee 'MMA · MUAY THAI · JIU-JITSU · S&C' band, and SKU-style program labels.
fontRecommendation.pairingRationale: Fraunces (editorial display) + Inter (utility sans) + JetBrains Mono (spec) is the prestige-fight-documentary kit. Fraunces brings the title-card weight that cinematic-fight needs without going full Playfair-luxury. Inter handles every interface element with crisp neutrality so the design feels modern and not period. JetBrains Mono on the credential blocks reads like a fight-record HUD — which is exactly the training-lab cross-reference we want for the instructor section.

designStyleNote: Not a single skill — bespoke synthesis. Bone structure from cinematic-fight (obsidian + portrait scale + film grain). Data layout from training-lab (instructor bento with credentials as spec). Typography discipline from taste-skill (editorial serif + utility sans + spec mono, strict 8-pt grid). Founder section pulls quiet craft from championship-heritage (warm tint, story-as-artist-statement, no bro energy).
